channel.title: Copley Family History

item.title: Roger Copley
item.description: Roger Copley of Roughley (1430–1490) Biographical Profile Born: 1430 Died: 1490 Location: Roughley (also Roughey), Surrey, England Title: Esquire Significance: Son of Sir Richard Copley; father of William Copley who established the Bredon Copley line; established Copley seat at Roughley, Surrey Fami...

item.title: Sources and Evidence Index
item.description: Central evidence backbone for the Copley Family Research Vault, linking major claims to source status, supporting pages, and next proof targets.

item.title: RQ-M5-PHASE-2-FINDINGS
item.description: RQ-M5 Research: Phase 2 Findings Search Kinawley Parish, County Fermanagh Date: April 23-25, 2026 Researcher: Claude Code / Claude for Chrome Question: Was Ann Munday actually Ann Murray? Critical Documentary Findings 1.

item.title: Changelog
item.description: Copley Family Research — Changelog A running record of significant additions, corrections, and improvements to this wiki.

item.title: RQ-M5 Research — Tithe Applotment Books Search (Kinawley, Fermanagh)
item.description: RQ-M5 Research: Tithe Applotment Books Search Research Question: Was “Ann Munday” (wife of Michael Copley Sr., b. c.1823, Kinawley, Co.

item.title: Father Thomas Copley - Dorsey Notes
item.description: Father Thomas Copley - Dorsey Notes Source: Katherine Fitzsimmons Dorsey, The Life of Father Thomas Copley: A Founder of Maryland (Georgetown, 1885) Available in text form at Calameo Why It Matters This book is a secondary synthesis, but it contains several useful clues about the Copley family’s pro...

item.title: Gatton and Leigh Place Notes
item.description: Gatton and Leigh Place Notes This page captures Tom Copley’s family-memory notes about the Gatton / Leigh Place / London Copley branch.

item.title: Sir Thomas Copley Letters Notes
item.description: Sir Thomas Copley Letters Notes Source: Richard Copley Christie, ed., Letters of Sir Thomas Copley, of Gatton, Surrey, and Roughey, Sussex, Knight and Baron in France, to Queen Elizabeth and Her Ministers: From the Originals in the Record Office and the British Museum (London: Chiswick Press, 1897; ...
